name: fourpt-runt-line-20260721
question: Why does 4pt/max (142 cols) fail restore where 3pt/max (194 cols) succeeds on the same corpus?

environment.harness: fourpt_cell.py (isolated single-cell copy of the earlier VM harness) + diag.py/repro.py

4pt_max_real_corpus.detail: deterministic; identical failure every rep: 'decode failed: corrected stream did not yield a valid group header'

4pt_max_random_matched_line_count.note: 209 encoded data lines matched to real corpus; passes -> encoded length alone is NOT the driver

line_diff_diagnostic.method: PDF text layer (ground truth) vs whitelist-constrained OCR transcript, space-canonicalized, per-page sequence alignment

benign_shared_artifacts: T-frame 'PAGE n/N' display suffix mangled (both corpora)

fatal_unique_damage.page2_line70_printed: LMYCLH C3AT #YYDV (runt final data line, 4 payload chars)
fatal_unique_damage.page2_line69: leading frame token 'LMYCMV' read as 'L ' (payload otherwise nearly clean)

random_control_passing_doc.final_data_line: near-full width; no runt; no fatal damage

minimal_repro.method: same random source file, pad with N x 0x5A bytes; only the final-line remainder changes

pad_3_bytes.result: FAIL: cannot recover RS parameters: data/parity line counts are inconsistent (missing or spurious lines)

root_cause: At small font sizes Tesseract psm-6 cannot segment/read a runt final data line (a few payload chars); it destroys that line and can corrupt the leading frame token of the line above. Whether a 4pt/max document fails is decided by encoded_length mod line_capacity - content matters only through that remainder. The two observed error messages are the two downstream symptoms of the same damage (runt read as garbage vs runt dropped entirely).
open_subquestions: Does an engineered runt also break 3pt/max and 4pt/60? (expected yes; untested)
Exact minimum safe final-line length per font size (a threshold sweep would bound it)
follow_up: eliminate runt final data lines at the encoder (implemented same day: the codec pads the protected stream so the final data line's printed payload is never below half the line width; header orig_len stays unpadded so decode truncates for free)
